arke_id.description: Arke ULID (derived from actor for regular users, required for admins)

permissions.description: Permission grants to apply to the new entity atomically

space_id.description: Space ULID — if provided, the entity is added to this space atomically

post.summary: Create a new entity, optionally adding it to a space and granting permissions
x-arke-rules: Requires write_level clearance >= entity's write_level
Requires read_level clearance >= entity's read_level
Non-admin actors are scoped to their own arke; admins must pass arke_id explicitly
If space_id is provided, requires contributor role or above on that space
All operations (create, space add, permission grants) are atomic
Only the owner, an entity admin, or a system admin may delete
Requires write_level clearance >= entity's write_level

get.summary: Fetch a single entity by ID
x-arke-rules: Requires read_level clearance >= entity's read_level
Returns 404 if entity is not visible to you
Only the owner, an entity editor, or an entity admin may update
Requires write_level clearance >= entity's write_level
Optimistic concurrency: must pass current ver to update

Only the owner, an entity editor, or an entity admin may change levels

direction.description: Whether this entity is the source (out) or target (in)

get.summary: List relationships for an entity
x-arke-rules: Results filtered by your classification clearance
Only shows relationships where you can read the relationship entity

permissions.description: Permission grants to apply to the new relationship entity atomically

read_level.description: Classification level for reading. Must be >= max(source, target) read_level. Defaults to that maximum.

space_id.description: Space ULID — if provided, the relationship entity is added to this space atomically

write_level.description: Classification level for writing. Must be >= max(source, target) write_level. Defaults to that maximum.

post.summary: Create a relationship from this entity to a target, optionally adding it to a space and granting permissions
x-arke-rules: Requires edit access on the source entity (owner, editor, or admin role)
Requires read access on the target entity
Relationship read_level must be >= max(source, target) read_level
Relationship write_level must be >= max(source, target) write_level
If space_id is provided, requires contributor role or above on that space
All operations (create, space add, permission grants) are atomic

x-arke-rules: You become the owner of the space

delete.summary: Soft-delete a space (set status=deleted, RLS: owner/admin)
x-arke-rules: Only the space owner or a system admin may delete

get.summary: Fetch a single space by ID
x-arke-rules: Requires read_level clearance >= space's read_level
Requires space role: editor or above
Owner and system admins bypass role checks

get.summary: List entities in a space
x-arke-rules: Requires read_level clearance >= space's read_level
Entity results further filtered by your classification clearance

post.summary: Add an entity to a space (RLS: contributor+)
x-arke-rules: Requires space role: contributor or above

delete.summary: Remove an entity from a space (RLS: editor+ or added_by)
x-arke-rules: You can remove entities you added yourself
Otherwise requires space role: editor or above

get.summary: List entity-access grants on a space
x-arke-rules: Requires read_level clearance >= space's read_level

post.summary: Grant entity-level access to all entities in a space. Accepts a single grant or bulk grants array
x-arke-rules: Requires space role: admin
Valid roles: editor, admin
Grantees can edit/admin any entity currently in the space
Removing an entity from the space revokes this access
Maximum 100 grants per request

delete.summary: Revoke entity-level access on a space
x-arke-rules: Requires space role: admin

get.summary: Get worker configuration (keys redacted)
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may access

api_key.description: New LLM API key (will be re-encrypted)

x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may update

get.summary: List invocation history for a worker
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocations

get.summary: Get the most recent invocation for a worker
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocations

post.description: Queues a worker invocation. Returns 202 with an invocation ID by default. Pass ?wait=true to block until the invocation completes (returns 200 with full result).

parameters.description: If 'true', block until invocation completes and return the full result
parameters.name: wait

schema.description: If 'true', block until invocation completes and return the full result

prompt.description: The task prompt for the worker

store_log.description: Override log storage (default: true, respects worker's log_level setting)

result.description: Structured result output from the worker

post.summary: Invoke a worker with a prompt
x-arke-rules: Requires owner, system admin, or invoker permission grant

get.summary: List permissions on a worker
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view permissions

grantee_id.description: Actor or group ULID to grant access to

post.summary: Grant invocation access on a worker (owner/admin only)
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may grant permissions

delete.summary: Revoke invocation access from an actor or group
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may revoke permissions

get.description: Retrieve the current state of a worker invocation. Use this to poll for completion after a 202 response from the invoke endpoint. Includes queue_position when status is 'queued'.

queue_position.description: Position in queue (1-based), null if not queued

get.summary: Get a worker invocation by ID (poll for status)
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocations

get.description: Retrieves all invocations in the tree rooted at the given invocation ID using a recursive query. Returns a flat array ordered by depth then timestamp, with parent_invocation_id for client-side tree building.
get.summary: Get the full invocation tree from a root invocation
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocation trees
